prompt: I want to start the design pipeline in guided mode. I already have research from our last round of user interviews — here's what we found: Data engineers spend 40% of their time on access request workflows. The current approval process requires 3-5 manual handoffs across teams. Users described it as 'death by email chain.' Top pain points: no visibility into request status, duplicate requests, and approvals getting lost. Key personas: Data Engineer (requestor), Data Owner (approver), Security Admin (auditor). The main opportunity is a self-service access request portal with automated routing.

assertions.text: Recognized guided mode from user prompt
Skipped Stage 1 research — did not re-run research
Built research report from user-provided findings (Stage 2)
Included checkpoint/pause markers at each stage transition
Research report uses SDS shell (header, sidenav)
Journey map based on the 3 provided personas
IA includes happy paths, edge cases, error states, and fringe cases
Research review evaluated designs against provided findings
